modules.module_title: Introduction to Laundry & Dry Cleaning Operations, Fabrics & Fibers
Laundry Chemicals, Detergents, Starch & Water Hardness
Sorting, Washing Cycle Settings, Bleaching & Fabric Softening
Dry Cleaning Processes, Solvent Safety (PERC) & Stain Removal
Drying, Ironing, Steam Pressing & Packaging of Finished Garments
Professional Ethics, Operations Management, Safety & Laundry Business Setup

questions.question: Laundry & Dry Cleaning Operations (dholaee aur shushk safai) me fabric sorting (kapdon ki chhantnee) ka pehla rule kya hota hai?

options.A: Kapdon ko color (whites vs darks) aur fabric type (cotton, wool, silk) ke according separate karna
options.B: Washing all clothes together regardless of color or fabric thickness
options.C: Sorting base on customer name alphabetically only

questions.explanation: Color bleeding and fabric damage prevent karne ke liye whites ko dark colors se aur delicate silks ko rough denims se alag karna laundry sorting baseline rule hai.

questions.question: Dry Cleaning me paani (water) ki jagah primary cleaning fluid ke roop me kis chemical solvent ko use kiya jata hai?

options.A: Perchloroethylene (Perc - shushk safai ka main solvent)

questions.explanation: Dry cleaning waterless process hai. Perchloroethylene (Perc) fibers ko bina expand/shrink kiye grease and stains dissolve kar deta hai.

questions.question: Silk aur Woolen (uni) delicate clothes ko wash karne ke liye kis type ke laundry detergents aur water temperature recommend hote hain?

options.A: Mild/neutral pH liquid detergents with cold water (thanda paani)
options.B: Strong alkaline powder detergents with boiling hot water

questions.explanation: Protein fibers (wool, silk) hot water aur harsh alkaline soaps me shrink aur weaken ho jate hain, isliye gentle cold washing zaroori hai.

questions.question: Laundry processes me 'Hard Water' (khara paani) use karne par cleaning efficiency par kya effect hota hai?

options.A: Reduces lather formation and leaves white soap scum deposits on fibers
options.D: Zero effect on detergents wash cycles

questions.explanation: Hard water ke calcium aur magnesium ions detergent soap molecules ke sath react karke insoluble soap scum (precipitate) banate hain jo cleaning ko bad banata hai.

questions.question: Garment care labels par 'Circle containing an F' (gole me F) symbol kya indicate karta hai dry cleaning me?

options.A: Dry clean using Petroleum solvents only (flammable solvents)
options.B: Wash under running water hose only

questions.explanation: Circle with F indicates hydrocarbon/petroleum solvents usage restrictions, saving fragile synthetic fabrics from damage by harsh Perc solvent.

questions.question: Washing machines me 'Extract Cycle' (spin cycle) ka kya role hota hai?

options.A: Uses centrifugal force to spin clothes rapidly and remove excess water before drying
options.B: Injecting bleaching powders into drum tub
options.C: Washing fibers with hot steam sprays

questions.explanation: Spin cycle high speed spinning se water drop squeezeout karta hai jo drying hours aur energy bills reduce checks karta hai.

questions.question: White cotton sheets me se high chlorine bleach applications yellow staining kyu cause karti hain?

options.A: Excessive chlorine breaks fabric chemical structures and leaves yellow chlorine deposit margins
options.C: Bleach evaporates and turns to sugar

questions.explanation: Over-bleaching cellulose fibers ko damage karti hai aur fibers ko oxidize karke pale yellow discoloration leave karti hai.

questions.question: Stain removal process me 'Ink stains' (masi ke nishaan) ko clean karne ke liye kya chemical treat suitable hai?

options.A: Rubbing alcohol (isopropyl alcohol) or methylated spirits

questions.explanation: Ink solvent dyes se banti hai jo alcohol me soluble hoti hai, alcohol application dye pigments ko bind karke extract checks kar leti hai.

questions.question: Laundry finishing setups 'Steam Pressing' (bhaap ki istree) hot iron dry press se better kyu hoti hai garments ke liye?

options.A: Steam relaxes fiber molecules faster without crushing fabric layers or causing heat burns shine marks
options.C: Steam press uses zero electric currents

questions.explanation: Steam moisture and heat blend fiber stress wrinkles ko flat release kar dete hain bina shiny heat marks leave kiye, highly essential for wool suits.

questions.question: Dry cleaning plants solvent safety rules checks me PERC solvent leaks detector alarms kyu lagaye jate hain?

options.A: PERC is toxic carcinogenic solvent (causes nervous system damages if high vapors are inhaled)
options.C: Detectors identify lost buttons in drum

questions.explanation: Perchloroethylene inhalation hazardous toxic risk guidelines checks ke under close space leak alarm units standard regulatory norms hain.

questions.question: Cotton shirts crisp sizing look dene ke liye finishing bath me kya product apply kiya jata hai?

options.A: Liquid starch (mand or revoking agent)

questions.explanation: Liquid starch cellulose fibers coat karta hai jo ironing heat under dry rigid profile structure aur shine yield karta hai.

questions.question: Laundry sorting checks me 'Care label checks' why first step of check in laundry plants?

options.A: Identifies legal dry clean only restrictions, washing temps limits and bleach safety rules

questions.explanation: Reading care icons prevents permanent fabric melt, bleed or shrink accidents during industrial washer loads.

questions.question: Fabric care definitions 'Colorfastness' what does it mean?

options.A: Ability of fabric dyes to resist fading or bleeding into water during washing cycles
options.B: Speed of fabric drying in sun

questions.explanation: High colorfastness fabrics loose dyes leak nahi karte, low colorfastness items require isolated individual wash paths.

questions.question: Laundry softeners operations 'Cationic surfactants' does softeners do what to fiber charges?

options.A: Neutralize negative charges on wet fibers, reducing static cling and leaving soft fiber touch feels

questions.explanation: Softener molecules coat fibers with thin lubricating fat layers, neutralizing static electricity locks.

questions.question: Laundry bookkeeping 'Tagging system' why color plastic pins/barcodes are pinned on cuffs?

options.A: Ensures clothes of different customers are tracked and sorted back correctly without mixup losses

questions.explanation: Individual barcode tagging prevents inventory losses during high volume bulk dryer and washer runs.

questions.question: Laundry sorting color whites?

options.A: Kapdon ko color (whites vs darks) aur fabric type (cotton, wool, silk) ke according separate karna
options.B: Washing all clothes together regardless of color or fabric thickness
options.C: Sorting base on customer name alphabetically only

questions.explanation: Color bleeding and fabric damage prevent karne ke liye whites ko dark colors se aur delicate silks ko rough denims se alag karna laundry sorting baseline rule hai.

questions.explanation: Liquid starch cellulose fibers coat karta hai jo ironing heat under dry rigid profile structure aur shine yield karta hai.

questions.explanation: Reading care icons prevents permanent fabric melt, bleed or shrink accidents during industrial washer loads.

options.A: Ability of fabric dyes to resist fading or bleeding into water during washing cycles
options.B: Speed of fabric drying in sun

questions.explanation: High colorfastness fabrics loose dyes leak nahi karte, low colorfastness items require isolated individual wash paths.

options.A: Ensures clothes of different customers are tracked and sorted back correctly without mixup losses

questions.explanation: Individual barcode tagging prevents inventory losses during high volume bulk dryer and washer runs.

questions.question: Laundry chemical checks 'Optical Brighteners' why added to commercial detergents?

options.A: Absorb UV light rays and emit blue fluorescent light (tricks human eye to see fabric whiter than normal)

questions.explanation: Optical brighteners deposit fluorescent dyes on fibers, masking natural yellow aging tones of white clothes.

questions.question: Garment care definitions 'Shrinkage limits' which fabrics show highest moisture shrinkage?

options.A: Unsanforized Wool and Cotton (protein/cellulose natural fibers swell, locking threads tight)

questions.explanation: Natural fibers contract when washed in hot waters with high mechanical agitation, demanding pre-shrunk checks.

questions.question: Stain chemistry 'Blood stains' what water temperature is strictly used to clean blood spots?

options.A: Cold water strictly (hot water cooks protein markers, setting stain permanently)

questions.explanation: Hemoglobin protein coagulates under heat, binding fibers permanently. Cold wash dissolves organic proteins easily.

questions.question: Dry cleaning solvent recovery 'Carbon filters absorption' why solvent vapor recovery is done?

options.A: Recycles expensive chemicals, reducing plant operational costs and avoiding air pollution venting

questions.explanation: Dry clean machines trap evaporated solvent vapors, cooling and filtering them to reuse, cutting chemical wastes.

questions.question: Laundry operations 'Flatwork ironer / Calendar machine' what item types does it process?

options.A: Flat sheets, table linens, pillowcases (large flat linen items pressed rapidly via heated rollers)

questions.explanation: Flatwork ironers automate industrial linens pressing, feeding flat sheets directly through heated roll drums.

questions.question: Optical Brighteners whiter blue?

options.A: Absorb UV light rays and emit blue fluorescent light (tricks human eye to see fabric whiter than normal)

questions.explanation: Optical brighteners deposit fluorescent dyes on fibers, masking natural yellow aging tones of white clothes.

questions.question: Drying shrinkage wool cotton?

options.A: Unsanforized Wool and Cotton (protein/cellulose natural fibers swell, locking threads tight)

questions.explanation: Natural fibers contract when washed in hot waters with high mechanical agitation, demanding pre-shrunk checks.

questions.question: Blood stains cold water?

options.A: Cold water strictly (hot water cooks protein markers, setting stain permanently)

questions.explanation: Hemoglobin protein coagulates under heat, binding fibers permanently. Cold wash dissolves organic proteins easily.

questions.question: Dry cleaning solvent recovery?

options.A: Recycles expensive chemicals, reducing plant operational costs and avoiding air pollution venting

questions.explanation: Dry clean machines trap evaporated solvent vapors, cooling and filtering them to reuse, cutting chemical wastes.

questions.question: Flatwork ironer sheets press?

options.A: Flat sheets, table linens, pillowcases (large flat linen items pressed rapidly via heated rollers)

questions.explanation: Flatwork ironers automate industrial linens pressing, feeding flat sheets directly through heated roll drums.

questions.question: Dry cleaning chemistry 'Potted stain chemicals' why different spotting agents are used?

options.A: Protein stains need enzymatic digesters, while paint/grease stains need amyl-acetate solvent drops
options.B: All stains dissolve under pure bleach drops
options.C: Soap water clears all spots easily

questions.explanation: Stain chemistry targets molecular structures. Careless chemical choice can set food/dye stains permanently.

questions.question: Dryer safety procedures 'Lint screen cleaning' how often is lint mesh cleaned to prevent fires?

options.A: After every drying load shift (dry lint is highly flammable and blocks hot air ducts)

questions.explanation: Fluffy cotton lint traps dryer heat. Keeping screens clean prevents static spark ignitions in ducts.

questions.question: Industrial washing safety 'Interlock switches' why installed on washer doors?

options.A: Prevents door from opening while drum spinner is rotating (stops spin accidents)

questions.explanation: High speed extraction drums carry massive momentum. Safety switches cut power instantly if doors are unlocked.

questions.question: Dry cleaning operations 'Distillation unit check' why dry clean solvent is boiled inside distillation stills?

options.A: Separates pure solvent from dissolved grease oils and dirt deposits collected from previous loads
options.C: Boiling solvent makes it smell sweet

questions.explanation: Boiling PERC vaporizes solvent, leaving heavy oils behind. Condensed pure solvent is reused in next washes.

questions.question: Laundry logistics 'Delivery garment covers' why thin plastic bags are draped over pressed coats?

options.A: Protects pressed fibers from outdoor humidity, dust and stain contacts during vehicle transit delivery

questions.explanation: Fine plastic hanger sleeves shield clean outfits from soot deposits and rain spots during customer hand-offs.

questions.question: Dry cleaning stain potting?

options.A: Protein stains need enzymatic digesters, while paint/grease stains need amyl-acetate solvent drops
options.B: All stains dissolve under pure bleach drops
options.C: Soap water clears all spots easily

questions.explanation: Stain chemistry targets molecular structures. Careless chemical choice can set food/dye stains permanently.

questions.question: Dryer lint safety fires?

options.A: After every drying load shift (dry lint is highly flammable and blocks hot air ducts)

questions.explanation: Fluffy cotton lint traps dryer heat. Keeping screens clean prevents static spark ignitions in ducts.

questions.question: Industrial washer door interlocks?

options.A: Prevents door from opening while drum spinner is rotating (stops spin accidents)

questions.explanation: High speed extraction drums carry massive momentum. Safety switches cut power instantly if doors are unlocked.

questions.question: Dry clean solvent distillation?

options.A: Separates pure solvent from dissolved grease oils and dirt deposits collected from previous loads
options.C: Boiling solvent makes it smell sweet

questions.explanation: Boiling PERC vaporizes solvent, leaving heavy oils behind. Condensed pure solvent is reused in next washes.

questions.question: Laundry delivery plastic covers?

options.A: Protects pressed fibers from outdoor humidity, dust and stain contacts during vehicle transit delivery

questions.explanation: Fine plastic hanger sleeves shield clean outfits from soot deposits and rain spots during customer hand-offs.

questions.question: Fabric finishing 'Pilling removal' (bhur hatana) what tool is used to clear fiber fuzz balls?

options.A: Fabric shaver or lint stone scraper

questions.explanation: Fiber shavers cut fuzzy pill locks off wool knits without tearing baseline knit fabrics.

questions.question: Laundry sorting safety 'Pocket check' why checking pants pockets is first task at loading?

options.A: Ink pens, metal coins, and matches left inside ruin garments, damage drums and trigger fires

questions.explanation: Pens leak ink inside heat dryers, staining entire laundry loads. Metal coins lock water drain pumps.

questions.question: Fabric softener formulations why they should be avoided on microfiber cloths and bath towels?

options.A: Wax coats fibers, dropping absorption capacity and rendering microfibers useless

questions.explanation: Silicon/fat coatings form hydrophobic barriers on fibers, blocking water absorption and destroying cleaning capacities.

questions.question: Dry cleaning solvent safety 'Flash Point' why petroleum solvents require low operating temperatures?

options.A: Low flash points mean solvents easily catch fire if heated near ignition sources

questions.explanation: Hydrocarbon petroleum cleaners are flammable. keeping wash temperatures low prevents solvent vapor explosions.

questions.question: Dry clean pressing setups 'Sleeve board' what clothing parts are pressed on it?

options.A: Narrow coat and shirt sleeves (allows ironing sleeve tube without pressing crease lines into fabric)

questions.explanation: Sleeve boards insert inside cuffs, enabling crease-free tube shapes, vital for professional suits.

questions.question: Fabric finishing bhur pill removal?

options.A: Fabric shaver or lint stone scraper

questions.explanation: Fiber shavers cut fuzzy pill locks off wool knits without tearing baseline knit fabrics.

questions.question: Laundry sorting pocket checks?

options.A: Ink pens, metal coins, and matches left inside ruin garments, damage drums and trigger fires

questions.explanation: Pens leak ink inside heat dryers, staining entire laundry loads. Metal coins lock water drain pumps.

questions.question: Softener microfibers towels restrictions?

options.A: Wax coats fibers, dropping absorption capacity and rendering microfibers useless

questions.explanation: Silicon/fat coatings form hydrophobic barriers on fibers, blocking water absorption and destroying cleaning capacities.

questions.question: Petroleum dry clean flash point safety?

options.A: Low flash points mean solvents easily catch fire if heated near ignition sources

questions.explanation: Hydrocarbon petroleum cleaners are flammable. keeping wash temperatures low prevents solvent vapor explosions.

questions.question: Dry clean press sleeve board?

options.A: Narrow coat and shirt sleeves (allows ironing sleeve tube without pressing crease lines into fabric)

questions.explanation: Sleeve boards insert inside cuffs, enabling crease-free tube shapes, vital for professional suits.

questions.question: Delicate fabrics care 'Angora / Cashmere wool storage' why cedar blocks are stored in clothes chests?

options.A: Cedar releases natural oils that repel carpet moths and beetles larvae from eating fibers

questions.explanation: Keratin fibers attract clothes moths. Cedar wood acts as natural insect repellent, preventing insect holes in wool.

questions.question: Laundry sanitizing checks 'Ozone laundry systems' what sanitizer gas is dissolved in cold washes?

options.A: Ozone (O3 - powerful disinfectant gas that allows cold washing sanitization)

questions.explanation: Ozone gas reacts with bacteria, sterilizing fabrics at cold temperatures and reducing hot water energy loads.

questions.question: Garment care labels 'Square with three vertical lines' what dry method does it describe?

options.A: Drip Dry (hang wet clothes straight without wringing out excess water)
options.B: Dry flat on horizontal tables only
options.C: Tumble dry at high heat settings

questions.explanation: Vertical lines icons specify drip drying. Wringing delicate synthetics ruins fabric structures, causing crease lines.

questions.question: Ironing safety procedures 'Thermal heat scale' what is maximum temp limit for nylon/acrylic?

options.A: 275 F (135 C) low heat (synthetics melt easily under high heat iron plates)

questions.explanation: Acrylic fibers have low heat resistance. Standard iron heat controls have synthetic zone labels to prevent melting.

questions.question: Dry cleaning logistics 'Garment sort barcode readers' why used during dispatch phases?

options.A: Groups different customer garments together automatically onto mechanical conveyor hanger tracks

questions.explanation: Conveyor automated barcodes sort finished hangers by client ID, streamlining customer dispatch systems.

questions.question: Angora cashmere storage cedar repels?

options.A: Cedar releases natural oils that repel carpet moths and beetles larvae from eating fibers

questions.explanation: Keratin fibers attract clothes moths. Cedar wood acts as natural insect repellent, preventing insect holes in wool.

questions.question: Ozone laundry cold water O3?

options.A: Ozone (O3 - powerful disinfectant gas that allows cold washing sanitization)

questions.explanation: Ozone gas reacts with bacteria, sterilizing fabrics at cold temperatures and reducing hot water energy loads.

questions.question: Drip Dry care label lines?

options.A: Drip Dry (hang wet clothes straight without wringing out excess water)
options.B: Dry flat on horizontal tables only
options.C: Tumble dry at high heat settings

questions.explanation: Vertical lines icons specify drip drying. Wringing delicate synthetics ruins fabric structures, causing crease lines.

questions.question: Ironing nylon synthetic low heat?

options.A: 275 F (135 C) low heat (synthetics melt easily under high heat iron plates)

questions.explanation: Acrylic fibers have low heat resistance. Standard iron heat controls have synthetic zone labels to prevent melting.

questions.question: Conveyor automated barcode dispatch?

options.A: Groups different customer garments together automatically onto mechanical conveyor hanger tracks

questions.explanation: Conveyor automated barcodes sort finished hangers by client ID, streamlining customer dispatch systems.

questions.question: Garment care labels 'A triangle with diagonal lines' what bleach safety does it indicate?

options.A: Only non-chlorine oxygen bleach is safe (do not use chlorine bleach)
options.B: Both chlorine and oxygen bleaches are banned

questions.explanation: Diagonal lines inside triangle notify users to avoid chlorine bleach, protecting dyes from fading.

questions.question: Laundry chemistry 'Enzymatic detergents' which enzymes are targeted to digest grass/blood stains?

options.A: Protease enzymes (breaks down protein molecule stains)

questions.explanation: Protease enzymes target protein structures (blood, baby food), dissolving them into simple water-soluble amino acids.

questions.question: Dry cleaning safety checks why 'Solvent recovery dryer temperature' is monitored?

options.A: High air temperature can trigger flash ignitions of solvent vapors in petroleum systems
options.C: Hot air turns polyester fibers yellow

questions.explanation: Petroleum dry clean safety requires keeping temperatures below solvent flash limits, preventing ignition risks.

questions.question: Ironing finishing rules 'Pressing dark woolen suits' why a pressing cloth is used?

options.A: Prevents iron plate from flattening wool fibers and leaving shiny hot marks
options.B: Moistens woolen fibers to shrink them
options.C: Protects iron plate from wool dye stains

questions.explanation: Pressing cloths act as thermal barriers. Direct iron contact compresses wool fibers, creating permanent shiny marks.

questions.question: Laundry operations bookkeeping 'Weight capacity ratios' how it affects washing machine life?

options.A: Overloading strains drum bearings, slips motor belts and leads to poor cleaning due to lack of fall drop room

questions.explanation: Correct load weights protect drum balance, enabling clothes to tumble and clean thoroughly.

questions.question: Care labels diagonal triangle?

options.A: Only non-chlorine oxygen bleach is safe (do not use chlorine bleach)
options.B: Both chlorine and oxygen bleaches are banned

questions.explanation: Diagonal lines inside triangle notify users to avoid chlorine bleach, protecting dyes from fading.

options.A: Protease enzymes (breaks down protein molecule stains)

questions.explanation: Protease enzymes target protein structures (blood, baby food), dissolving them into simple water-soluble amino acids.

questions.question: Dry clean drying safety?

options.A: High air temperature can trigger flash ignitions of solvent vapors in petroleum systems
options.C: Hot air turns polyester fibers yellow

questions.explanation: Petroleum dry clean safety requires keeping temperatures below solvent flash limits, preventing ignition risks.

questions.question: Ironing wool pressing cloth?

options.A: Prevents iron plate from flattening wool fibers and leaving shiny hot marks
options.B: Moistens woolen fibers to shrink them
options.C: Protects iron plate from wool dye stains

questions.explanation: Pressing cloths act as thermal barriers. Direct iron contact compresses wool fibers, creating permanent shiny marks.

questions.question: Laundry machine overloading strain?

options.A: Overloading strains drum bearings, slips motor belts and leads to poor cleaning due to lack of fall drop room

questions.explanation: Correct load weights protect drum balance, enabling clothes to tumble and clean thoroughly.

questions.question: Washing cycle settings 'Delicate Cycle' how drum movement and spin speed change?

options.A: Slow intermittent drum rotation with low spin speeds (reduces fiber stretching/wear)
options.B: High speed continuous spin cycles hamesha
options.C: Drum rotates in one direction only at maximum speed

questions.explanation: Delicate washing cycles reduce mechanical friction, protecting fine lace and knit garments from fraying.

questions.question: Commercial wash formulations 'Souring agent' what pH level does it restore to fabrics?

options.A: Slightly acidic pH (restores fabric pH to 5.5 to 6.5, neutralizing alkaline detergent residues)

questions.explanation: Souring agents are mild acids added to rinse baths, neutralizing leftover detergent alkalis to prevent skin irritations.

questions.question: Garment care labels 'A hand inside washing tub' what wash temperature limits apply?

options.A: Hand wash only, in lukewarm water maximum (usually up to 30 C or 85 F)

questions.explanation: Hand wash symbols advise against machine washing, protecting fragile knit wools and silks from damage.

questions.question: Ironing techniques 'Steam bursts' why used on thick cotton/linen garments?

options.A: Moisture swells fibers and iron heat presses them instantly, setting crisp flat shapes
options.C: Steam speeds up iron cooling rates

questions.explanation: Linen and cotton fibers hold strong hydrogen bonds that resist dry iron presses. Steam swells them to flatten wrinkles.

questions.question: Dry cleaning business operations 'Environment clearances' why PERC waste management is monitored?

options.A: PERC is toxic groundwater pollutant (demands licensed waste disposal to prevent soil pollution)

questions.explanation: Regulatory laws mandate tracking dry cleaning sludge outputs, requiring approved disposals to avoid penalties.

questions.question: Delicate wash drum speed?

options.A: Slow intermittent drum rotation with low spin speeds (reduces fiber stretching/wear)
options.B: High speed continuous spin cycles hamesha
options.C: Drum rotates in one direction only at maximum speed

questions.explanation: Delicate washing cycles reduce mechanical friction, protecting fine lace and knit garments from fraying.

options.A: Hand wash only, in lukewarm water maximum (usually up to 30 C or 85 F)

questions.explanation: Hand wash symbols advise against machine washing, protecting fragile knit wools and silks from damage.

questions.question: Steam bursts cotton linen?

options.A: Moisture swells fibers and iron heat presses them instantly, setting crisp flat shapes
options.C: Steam speeds up iron cooling rates

questions.explanation: Linen and cotton fibers hold strong hydrogen bonds that resist dry iron presses. Steam swells them to flatten wrinkles.

options.A: PERC is toxic groundwater pollutant (demands licensed waste disposal to prevent soil pollution)

questions.explanation: Regulatory laws mandate tracking dry cleaning sludge outputs, requiring approved disposals to avoid penalties.

questions.question: Laundry & Dry Cleaning Operations final launch audits why machine calibration is key final step?

options.A: Ensures temperature controls, solvent pump pressures and spin rates are fully functional to prevent fabric damage

questions.explanation: Running final equipment calibrations verifies temperature limits and spin cycles, protecting customer garments.
